posts.text:
Overseas investors are piling into US stocks:

Foreign investors now allocate a record 63% of their US financial assets to equities.

This percentage has more than DOUBLED since the 2008 Financial Crisis.

This also surpasses the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble peak by ~10 percentage points.

By comparison, the long-term average allocation is ~40%.

As a result, foreign investors now own a record $21.3 trillion in US stocks and equity funds, surging +170% since 2020.

Foreign investors are doubling down on US markets.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
It’s so exciting to be exploring on the leading edge of nanotechnology…  it’s just like looking out into a night sky and pondering what lies beyond.  

It’s fascinating to try to comprehend why atoms and electrons behave as they do.  

How to work with them and influence the to behave the way we want them to.  

As investors and partners with $hg $hgraf we are unlocking a new deeper understanding of how matter can be manipulated and new materials can be synthesized. 

The results that are being achieved are profound.  5 hours locked in a room with Dr Sorensen has me both elated and exhausted.  

We are going after some of the biggest problems in the world to map out viable solutions that will he achieved due to turbostratic fractal graphene.  It’s a world changing material.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
